title: ricinus communis var. leucocarpus
intro: Ricinus communis var. leucocarpus, also known as the white castor bean or white castor oil plant, is a variety of the Ricinus communis plant, which is native to tropical Africa and is widely cultivated for its medicinal and industrial properties. The plant's seeds contain ricin, a toxic compound, but the oil extracted from its seeds has medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The oil has been used to treat various health conditions, including skin conditions, wound healing, and menstrual disorders, and has also been used to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. Ricinus communis var. leucocarpus oil can be prepared in various forms, including capsules, tinctures, and topical creams, and is often used in traditional medicine and herbal remedies.
